items.briefing:
With the FIFA World Cup 2026 underway, cybercriminals are targeting fans through fake ticketing sites, phishing emails, and fraudulent streaming offers — Australians following the tournament online should be especially cautious. Separately, a new ESET report found 45% of small and medium businesses experienced a cyber incident recently, a reminder that organisations of all sizes remain at real risk.

**Practical tip:** If you're searching for World Cup streams or ticket deals, only use official, well-known websites — if a deal looks too good to be true, it almost certainly is.

featured.title: ESET report finds 45% of SMBs hit by cyber incidents
featured.quote: Nearly half of Australian SMBs suffered cyber incidents, revealing critical security gaps in local businesses.
